seo.title: Building Research Agents with AG2 + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build research agents with AG2 + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building Research Agents with AG2 enables sophisticated multi-agent workflows for web search, analysis, and synthesis, leveraging enhanced group chat capabilities. Guardrails are essential to prevent conversation drift, factual inaccuracies, and biased outputs in these high-stakes information pipelines. Vex integration provides real-time verification for source credibility, claim accuracy, and recency, ensuring reliable research outputs.

architectureOverview.heading: Research Agents Architecture with AG2
architectureOverview.description:
AG2 Research Agents typically feature a coordinator agent that orchestrates a searcher agent for web queries, an analyzer for fact extraction, and a synthesizer for summarization. The searcher uses tools like SerpAPI or browser automation to fetch sources, while the analyzer performs claim verification and bias detection. Group chat consensus ensures balanced outputs, but requires safeguards against speaker bias and off-topic drift.

The workflow follows a sequential pipeline: search → verify → analyze → synthesize, with cross-agent validation at each step. Vex hooks intercept messages to enforce recency checks (e.g., sources < 6 months old) and source reputation scoring. This architecture scales to complex topics but amplifies risks like hallucinated consensus on outdated facts.

risks.name: Agent conversation spiraling off-topic
risks.description: Research agents debate tangential topics instead of focusing on query parameters
risks.mitigation: Vex topic drift detection with query relevance scoring

risks.name: Group chat consensus on incorrect facts
risks.description: Majority agents agree on hallucinated or outdated information
risks.mitigation: Vex claim verification against primary sources

risks.name: Outdated source recency violations
risks.description: Agents cite sources older than acceptable threshold for topic
risks.mitigation: Vex automated recency checks with configurable windows

risks.name: Speaker selection bias in multi-agent groups
risks.description: Dominant agents suppress minority fact-checks during synthesis
risks.mitigation: Vex balanced speaker rotation and dissent amplification

implementation.title: Basic AG2 Research Agent Group
implementation.description: Set up core multi-agent research workflow with searcher and synthesizer
implementation.code:
from ag2 import GroupChat, GroupChatManager
from ag2.agent import AssistantAgent

searcher = AssistantAgent(
    name="Searcher",
    system_message="You are a web research expert. Use SerpAPI to find recent, credible sources.",
    llm_config={"config_list": [{"model": "gpt-4o", "api_key": os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")}]}
)

analyzer = AssistantAgent(
    name="Analyzer",
    system_message="Verify claims, check source credibility, detect bias.",
    llm_config={"config_list": [{"model": "gpt-4o", "api_key": os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")}]}
)

groupchat = GroupChat(agents=[searcher, analyzer], messages=[], max_round=12)
manager = GroupChatManager(groupchat=groupchat)

result = manager.initiate_chat(
    None,
    message="Research latest developments in quantum computing error correction"
)

implementation.title: SerpAPI Tool Integration for Searcher
implementation.description: Configure searcher agent with real web search capabilities

implementation.title: Source Verification Function
implementation.description: Analyzer agent with source credibility scoring

implementation.title: Group Chat with Termination Conditions
implementation.description: Configure research group with consensus and timeout safeguards
implementation.code:
groupchat = GroupChat(
    agents=[searcher, analyzer, synthesizer],
    messages=[],
    max_round=15,
    speaker_selection_method="auto",  # AG2 enhanced selection
    allow_repeat_speaker=False,
    select_speaker_sep="\n###",
    terminate_on=["RESEARCH_COMPLETE", "CONSENSUS_REACHED"]
)

manager = GroupChatManager(
    groupchat=groupchat,
    llm_config={"config_list": [{"model": "gpt-4o", "api_key": os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")}]}
)

result = manager.initiate_chat(
    None,
    message="Provide verified summary of mRNA vaccine efficacy data from 2024 studies"
)

vexIntegration.title: Register Vex Message Hook on Agents
vexIntegration.description: Install and configure Vex SDK as message interceptor for all research agents
vexIntegration.code:
import vex
from ag2.agent import AssistantAgent

# Initialize Vex client
vex_client = vex.Client(api_key=os.getenv("VEX_API_KEY"))

# Define verification policies for research use case
policies = {
    "source_verification": "medium",
    "claim_accuracy": "high",
    "recency_window": "6m",
    "bias_detection": "enabled"
}

# Register Vex as message hook
@vex_client.hook(policies=policies)
def research_guardrail(message: str, agent_name: str, context: dict) -> str:
    """Vex verification for research messages"""
    verdict = vex_client.verify(
        message,
        context={
            "agent_role": agent_name,
            "use_case": "research",
            "sources": context.get('sources', [])
        }
    )
    
    if not verdict.approved:
        return f"[VEX_BLOCKED] {verdict.reason}: {message}"
    return message

# Apply to all agents
searcher.register_reply([research_guardrail], config=None)
analyzer.register_reply([research_guardrail], config=None)

print("Vex guardrails registered on research agents")

vexIntegration.title: Real-time Source Verification Hook
vexIntegration.description: Intercept search results for automatic credibility scoring

vexIntegration.title: Claim Accuracy and Bias Detection
vexIntegration.description: Final synthesis guardrail with claim verification and bias scoring
vexIntegration.code:
@vex_client.hook(policy="claim_accuracy")
def verify_synthesis(message: str, agent_name: str, context: dict) -> str:
    """Final verification before research output"""
    claims = vex.extract_claims(message)
    verification_results = []
    
    for claim in claims[:3]:  # Verify top 3 claims
        evidence = context.get('verified_sources', [])
        result = vex_client.verify_claim(
            claim,
            evidence=evidence,
            recency_window="6m"
        )
        status = "✅ VERIFIED" if result['confidence'] > 0.8 else "⚠️  NEEDS REVIEW"
        verification_results.append(f"{claim}\n{status} (confidence: {result['confidence']:.2f})")
    
    bias_score = vex_client.detect_bias(message)
    bias_warning = f"Bias score: {bias_score:.2f}" if bias_score > 0.3 else ""
    
    return f"""VERIFICATION SUMMARY:
{chr(10).join(verification_results)}

{bias_warning}

FINAL SYNTHESIS:
{message}"""

synthesizer.register_reply([verify_synthesis])

cta.heading: Secure Your AG2 Research Agents
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install ag2 vex-sdk` and protect your research pipeline from factual errors and bias.
